req.title: Card freeze / unfreeze (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers card freeze/unfreeze with both positive and negative paths. I designed tests covering: (1) a full freeze→authorize→unfreeze→authorize state cycle (STC), (2) authorization rejection on a frozen card (EP/positive), (3) freeze attempt on a non-existent card returning 404 (NEG), (4) authorization on a frozen card with insufficient funds—double-negative compounding (DT), and (5) rapid toggle at the boundary of allowed state transitions (BVA). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that provision and teardown cards, and all HTTP calls target /cards/{id}/freeze and the authorization endpoint.

tests.name: test_freeze_active_card_returns_200_and_updates_state
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Card exists and belongs to caller
tests.executable:
def test_freeze_active_card_returns_200_and_updates_state(auth_headers, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]
    r = <api_post>(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", headers=auth_headers)
    assert r.status_code == 200
    assert r.json()["status"] == "FROZEN"

    r2 = <api_get>(f"/cards/{card_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert r2.json()["status"] == "FROZEN"

    r3 = <api_post>(f"/cards/{card_id}/unfreeze", headers=auth_headers)
    assert r3.status_code == 200
    assert r3.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE"

tests.name: test_authorization_rejected_on_frozen_card
preconditions: Card exists, belongs to caller, and is in FROZEN state
actions: POST /authorizations with a valid transaction payload referencing the frozen card
POST /cards/{id}/unfreeze after assertion to restore state
expected: No debit applied to account balance
trace: R-FIN-060: frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts
tests.executable:
def test_authorization_rejected_on_frozen_card(auth_headers, frozen_card):
    card_id = frozen_card["id"]
    payload = <build_auth_payload>(card_id=card_id, amount=10_00)
    r = <api_post>("/authorizations", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert r.status_code in (402, 403)
    body = r.json()
    assert body.get("decline_reason") == "CARD_FROZEN"

    balance_before = frozen_card["account_balance"]
    bal_r = <api_get>(f"/accounts/{frozen_card['account_id']}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert bal_r.json()["balance"] == balance_before

    <api_post>(f"/cards/{card_id}/unfreeze", headers=auth_headers)

tests.name: test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404
preconditions: Card ID used does not exist in the system
expected: Response body contains error code CARD_NOT_FOUND
trace: R-FIN-060: error pathway — 404 when card does not exist
tests.executable:
def test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404(auth_headers):
    nonexistent_id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
    r = <api_post>(f"/cards/{nonexistent_id}/freeze", headers=auth_headers)
    assert r.status_code == 404
    body = r.json()
    assert body.get("error") == "CARD_NOT_FOUND"

tests.name: test_frozen_card_authorization_with_insufficient_funds_returns_compound_decline
actions: POST /authorizations with amount > 0 against frozen card with zero balance
expected: decline_reason is CARD_FROZEN (freeze takes precedence over funds check)
trace: R-FIN-060: negative paths — insufficient funds AND frozen card combined
R-FIN-060: frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts
tests.executable:
def test_frozen_card_authorization_with_insufficient_funds_returns_compound_decline(
    auth_headers, zero_balance_frozen_card
):
    card_id = zero_balance_frozen_card["id"]
    payload = <build_auth_payload>(card_id=card_id, amount=1_00)
    r = <api_post>("/authorizations", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert r.status_code in (402, 403)
    body = r.json()
    assert body.get("decline_reason") == "CARD_FROZEN"

    bal_r = <api_get>(
        f"/accounts/{zero_balance_frozen_card['account_id']}",
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert bal_r.json()["balance"] == 0

    <api_post>(f"/cards/{card_id}/unfreeze", headers=auth_headers)

tests.name: test_rapid_freeze_unfreeze_toggle_boundary_idempotency
expected: First freeze returns HTTP 200 with status FROZEN
Second freeze returns HTTP 200 or 409 — must NOT corrupt state
First unfreeze returns HTTP 200 with status ACTIVE
Second unfreeze returns HTTP 200 or 409 — must NOT corrupt state
trace: R-FIN-060: boundary — idempotent toggle at state edges
tests.executable:
def test_rapid_freeze_unfreeze_toggle_boundary_idempotency(auth_headers, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]

    r1 = <api_post>(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", headers=auth_headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 200
    assert r1.json()["status"] == "FROZEN"

    r2 = <api_post>(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", headers=auth_headers)
    assert r2.status_code in (200, 409)
    state_r = <api_get>(f"/cards/{card_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert state_r.json()["status"] == "FROZEN"

    r3 = <api_post>(f"/cards/{card_id}/unfreeze", headers=auth_headers)
    assert r3.status_code == 200
    assert r3.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE"

    r4 = <api_post>(f"/cards/{card_id}/unfreeze", headers=auth_headers)
    assert r4.status_code in (200, 409)
    final_r = <api_get>(f"/cards/{card_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert final_r.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE"
